title: AI agents explained: Build your first agent in 8 minutes
summary: Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) is a tool for building autonomous AI agents with multi-agent capabilities. The video demonstrates building a self-correcting multi-agent system using the ReAct framework and multiple agent patterns including sequential, reactive, and planning approaches. The practical example shows a Blog Writing Agent with validation and auto-correction features.
writeup: The video walks through building a first agent with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK), a tool for constructing autonomous, multi-agent systems. It covers the ReAct framework and several agent patterns: sequential, reactive, planning, and loop agents. The worked example is a Blog Writing Agent that adds validation and auto-correction, demonstrating how a multi-agent setup can self-correct its own output.
